title: [Feature]: Evaluate push-consumer support without weakening ACK semantics
problem: The core runner intentionally uses pull consumers for controlled batching and backpressure. NATS also supports push consumers, but they introduce different flow-control and concurrency behavior. Supporting push consumers might help specific deployments, but only if commit-then-ACK and bounded in-flight work remain enforceable.
proposal: Evaluate push-consumer support as a separate runner mode and document the decision. The evaluation should explain why pull consumers remain the preferred production default, which safety properties a future push mode must preserve, and which follow-up implementation work should be tracked separately.
users: NATS users who already operate push consumer patterns and maintainers evaluating broader JetStream compatibility.
delivery_semantics: Push mode must ACK only after durable sink success, must bound in-flight work, and must avoid unbounded memory growth. If these cannot be guaranteed, the feature should remain unsupported with documented rationale.
security: The feature must not require broader permissions than necessary and must not log payloads or sensitive metadata when flow-control errors occur.
acceptance: A push-consumer design decision is documented.
Recommended implementation work is split into separate feature requests.
Pull mode remains the documented default.
Delivery-contract test requirements cover ACK ordering in push mode.
tests: Run backlog manifest validation, Markdown link checks, focused backlog and documentation tests, and scripts/check.sh after the evaluation documentation and follow-up backlog items are in place.
documentation: Update docs/push-consumer-evaluation.md, docs/architecture.md, docs/commit-then-ack.md, docs/configuration.md, docs/operations.md, docs/testing.md, docs/nats-feature-gap-analysis.md, ROADMAP.md, docs/roadmap.md, and CHANGELOG.md.
closeout: Close with documented decision evidence after the evaluation findings are posted and follow-up feature requests are created.
